content.content:
## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>
Create a document with a cookie recipe.

response.delta: I'll create a classic chocolate chip cookie recipe document for you.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:

</skill>
Create a document with a cookie recipe.

content.text: I'll create a classic chocolate chip cookie recipe document for you.

params.name: Classic Chocolate Chip Cookies
params.content:
# Classic Chocolate Chip Cookies

A timeless recipe for soft, chewy chocolate chip cookies with crispy edges. Makes about 24 cookies.

## Ingredients

- 2¼ cups (280g) all-purpose flour
- 1 tsp baking soda
- 1 tsp salt
- 1 cup (225g) unsalted butter, softened
- ¾ cup (150g) granulated sugar
- ¾ cup (165g) packed brown sugar
- 2 large eggs
- 2 tsp vanilla extract
- 2 cups (340g) semi-sweet chocolate chips

## Instructions

1. **Preheat** your oven to 375°F (190°C). Line two baking sheets with parchment paper.
2. **Mix dry ingredients:** In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
3. **Cream butter and sugars:** In a large bowl, beat the softened butter with both sugars until light and fluffy, about 2–3 minutes.
4. **Add eggs and vanilla:** Beat in the eggs one at a time, then stir in the vanilla extract.
5. **Combine:** Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients, mixing until just combined. Don't overmix.
6. **Fold in chocolate chips:** Stir in the chocolate chips until evenly distributed.
7. **Scoop:** Drop rounded tablespoons of dough onto the prepared baking sheets, spacing them about 2 inches apart.
8. **Bake:** Bake for 9–11 minutes, or until the edges are golden but the centers are still soft.
9. **Cool:** Let the cookies rest on the baking sheet for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.

## Tips

- For extra chewy cookies, chill the dough for 30 minutes before baking.
- Add a sprinkle of flaky sea salt on top before baking for a gourmet touch.
- Store in an airtight container for up to 5 days, or freeze the dough for up to 3 months.

**Enjoy! 🍪**

response.delta:
've created a **Classic Chocolate Chip Cookies** recipe document for you! 🍪

The recipe includes:
- **Ingredients** — with

both cup and gram measurements for accuracy
- **Step-by-step instructions** — from preheating to cooling
- **Helpful tips** — for

chewier cookies, a gourmet touch, and storage guidance

The recipe makes about 24 soft and chewy cookies.
Let me know if you'd like me to adjust the recipe (like making it gluten-free, adding n
uts, or scaling the batch size) or create additional recipes!

tools.description: Opens and reads the contents of a new markdown document.

doc.description:
The URI of the referenced object. Protocols accepted:
- echo://01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4. 
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)
- dxn:org.dxos.type.calendar:0.1.0 -- named entity from the registry. Absoutely make sure it actually exists in the registry.

URIs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.

tools.name: update
tools.description: Applies a set of edits to the markdown document.

doc.description:
The URI of the referenced object. Protocols accepted:
- echo://01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4. 
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)
- dxn:org.dxos.type.calendar:0.1.0 -- named entity from the registry. Absoutely make sure it actually exists in the registry.

URIs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.

oldString.description: The text to find in the document. Set to undefined to append the newString to the end of the document.

newString.description: The text to replace it with.

replaceAll.description: If true, replaces all occurrences. Defaults to false (first occurrence only).

edits.description: The edits to apply to the document. Each edit finds oldString and replaces it with newString; omit oldString to append newString to the end.

content.content:
## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

## Context Objects

<object>
  <dxn>echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTQC</dxn>
  <typename>org.dxos.type.document</typename>
</object>
Add milk to the shopping list.

content.text: I'll take a look at the current document first.

content.content: I've added milk to your shopping list.
Add bread to the shopping list.
I've added bread to your shopping list.
Add eggs to the shopping list.
